title.en: Ensure GDM screen locks when the user is idle

prose.en: Ensure GDM screen locks when the user is idle

description.en: GNOME Desktop Manager can make the screen lock automatically whenever the user is idle for some amount of time.

rationale.en: Setting a lock\-out value reduces the window of opportunity for unauthorized user access to another user's session that has been left unattended.

audit.en:
**\- IF \-** A user profile is already created run the following commands to enable screen locks when the user is idle:  

**Note:**  

- `gsettings` commands in this section MUST be done from a command window on a graphical desktop or an error will be returned.
- The system must be restarted after all `gsettings` configurations have been set in order for CIS\-CAT Assessor to appropriately assess.

**\- OR/IF\-** A user profile does not exist:  

1. Create or edit the user profile in the `/etc/dconf/profile/` and verify it includes the following:

**Note:** `local` is the name of a dconf database used in the examples.  

1. Create the directory `/etc/dconf/db/local.d/` if it doesn't already exist:
2. Create the key file `/etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-screensaver` to provide information for the `local` database:

*Example key file:*  

**Note:** You must include the uint32 along with the integer key values as shown.  

1. Run the following command to update the system databases:

1. Users must log out and back in again before the system\-wide settings take effect.

```
# dconf update
```

```
# Specify the dconf path
[org/gnome/desktop/session]

# Number of seconds of inactivity before the screen goes blank
# Set to 0 seconds if you want to deactivate the screensaver.
idle-delay=uint32 180

# Specify the dconf path
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]

# Number of seconds after the screen is blank before locking the screen
lock-delay=uint32 0
```

```
user-db:user
system-db:{NAME_OF_DCONF_DATABASE}
```

```
# gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 900
```

```
# gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-delay 5
```

remediation.en:
**\- IF \-** A user profile is already created run the following commands to enable screen locks when the user is idle:  

**Note:**  

- `gsettings` commands in this section MUST be done from a command window on a graphical desktop or an error will be returned.
- The system must be restarted after all `gsettings` configurations have been set in order for CIS\-CAT Assessor to appropriately assess.

**\- OR/IF\-** A user profile does not exist:  

1. Create or edit the user profile in the `/etc/dconf/profile/` and verify it includes the following:

**Note:** `local` is the name of a dconf database used in the examples.  

1. Create the directory `/etc/dconf/db/local.d/` if it doesn't already exist:
2. Create the key file `/etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-screensaver` to provide information for the `local` database:

*Example key file:*  

**Note:** You must include the uint32 along with the integer key values as shown.  

1. Run the following command to update the system databases:

1. Users must log out and back in again before the system\-wide settings take effect.

```
# dconf update
```

```
# Specify the dconf path
[org/gnome/desktop/session]

# Number of seconds of inactivity before the screen goes blank
# Set to 0 seconds if you want to deactivate the screensaver.
idle-delay=uint32 180

# Specify the dconf path
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]

# Number of seconds after the screen is blank before locking the screen
lock-delay=uint32 0
```

```
user-db:user
system-db:{NAME_OF_DCONF_DATABASE}
```

```
# gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 900
```

```
# gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-delay 5
```
